questions.q: What is the product formed when butan-2-one reacts with Tollens' reagent?
questions.answer: Butan-2-one does not react with Tollens' reagent as it is a ketone and does not have an aldehyde group.

questions.q: Arrange the following in increasing order of their reactivity towards nucleophilic addition: acetone, acetophenone, benzaldehyde. Give reason.
questions.answer: The increasing order of reactivity is: acetophenone < acetone < benzaldehyde. This is due to the +I effect of the phenyl group in acetophenone and the -I effect of the phenyl group in benzaldehyde, which makes the carbonyl carbon more electron-deficient and hence more reactive.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The carbonyl group of aldehydes and ketones is ___________.
State whether the following statement is true or false: 'All aldehydes and ketones undergo Cannizzaro reaction.'
questions.answer: False, only aldehydes that do not have an α-hydrogen atom undergo Cannizzaro reaction.

questions.q: What is the product formed when propanal reacts with hydroxylamine?
questions.answer: Propanal reacts with hydroxylamine to form an oxime.

questions.q: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their acidity: formic acid, acetic acid, chloroacetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid. Give reason.
questions.answer: The decreasing order of acidity is: trifluoroacetic acid > chloroacetic acid > formic acid > acetic acid. This is due to the -I effect of the halogen atoms, which makes the O-H bond more polar and hence more acidic.

questions.q: Choose the correct answer: What is the product formed when acetone reacts with sodium nitrite and dilute hydrochloric acid? (A) Chloroacetone (B) Nitroacetone (C) Acetone oxime (D) None of these
questions.answer: (D) None of these, as acetone does not react with sodium nitrite and dilute hydrochloric acid.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The reaction of aldehydes and ketones with Grignard reagents gives ___________.
State whether the following statement is true or false: 'Benzaldehyde is more reactive than formaldehyde towards nucleophilic addition.'
questions.answer: False, formaldehyde is more reactive than benzaldehyde towards nucleophilic addition due to the -I effect of the phenyl group in benzaldehyde.

questions.q: What is the product formed when butanal reacts with ammonia?
questions.answer: Butanal reacts with ammonia to form an imine or a Schiff's base.
